policy.main_claim: AANA makes agents more auditable, safer, more grounded, and more controllable.
policy.not_raw_engine_claim: AANA is not yet proven as a raw agent-performance engine.

benchmark_reports.summary: AANA is reported as a runtime action-gating architecture evaluated on externally sourced traces with policy-derived labels.
limitations: Labels were policy-derived rather than benchmark-maintainer labels.
The result supports a narrow action-routing claim, not a broad alignment or production-certification claim.
wins: Measured action-gating comparisons showed stronger audit/control behavior than permissive or static guardrail baselines on the reported trace artifact.

benchmark_reports.summary: Probe-enabled tau2 runs are engineering diagnostics for finding planner failure modes and are not public AANA performance claims.
limitations: Probe planners may contain exact task literals or discovered recovery flows.
Probe metrics must not be merged into public AANA benchmark claims.

benchmark_reports.summary: Default scaffold runs without benchmark probes can be used as engineering evidence, but small smoke tests are not leaderboard claims.
limitations: Small smoke tests are useful for regression detection only.
Leaderboard or peer-review claims require maintainer-approved protocol and full reported artifacts.
